thesis_status_reasoning: All critical parameters are well within safe operating ranges following apparent resolution of a demand incident. Sector 4 pressure at 55 PSI is significantly above the 20 PSI contamination threshold, grid average pressure of 60 PSI exceeds the 25 PSI collapse threshold, and flow has normalized to 6000 GPM. The operator log explicitly confirms incident resolution and indicates the system performed as designed, with gravity reserves deploying during the event (evidenced by the 58% remaining level requiring overnight replenishment) and now safely returning to baseline operations.
action_reasoning: System has successfully weathered an asymmetric load event and is recovering normally. All falsification criteria remain comfortably unsatisfied, and the gravity reserve depletion to 58% demonstrates the redundancy architecture functioned exactly as the thesis predicts.

key_signals.signal: Sector 4 pressure at 55 PSI, 175% above critical 20 PSI contamination threshold
Gravity reserve at 58% after incident deployment, demonstrating successful passive failover during demand spike
System flow normalized to 6000 GPM from what was likely a significantly higher demand event
Pump cavitation sensor reading NORMAL with pumps operating at 50% load, indicating mechanical systems protected
Operator confirmation of incident resolution and planned reserve replenishment protocol

biggest_uncertainty: Lack of temporal data showing the system's behavior during the peak of the incident that depleted reserves from presumed full capacity to 58%, which would validate the automated protection and failover sequencing described in the thesis
